title: infinite loop on 'podman push <image ref>'
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

Over the last couple of days, all of my `podman push` attempts, with multiple images and trying to push to both quay.io and docker.io, seem to get in a recursive lop copying blobs

Small snippets like this:

```
Copying blob 8041d704d7cd [===>----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 121.9MiB
Copying blob 523e183c0552 [=======>------------------------------] 11.0MiB / 54.7MiB
Copying blob 6ccd2383e2e9 done  
Copying blob 3c2db4036f06 done  
Copying blob b4fa6ff1346d [==>-----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 172.7MiB
Copying blob 6e27b2228f9e done  
Copying blob 0b1b3a6418ad done  
Copying blob 2dc4374fcf9b done  
Copying blob 8041d704d7cd [===>----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 121.9MiB
Copying blob 523e183c0552 [=======>------------------------------] 11.0MiB / 54.7MiB
Copying blob 6ccd2383e2e9 done  
Copying blob 3c2db4036f06 done  
Copying blob b4fa6ff1346d [==>-----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 172.7MiB
Copying blob 6e27b2228f9e done  
Copying blob 0b1b3a6418ad done  
Copying blob 2dc4374fcf9b done  
Copying blob 8041d704d7cd [===>----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 121.9MiB
Copying blob 523e183c0552 [=======>------------------------------] 11.0MiB / 54.7MiB
Copying blob 6ccd2383e2e9 done  
Copying blob 3c2db4036f06 done  
Copying blob b4fa6ff1346d [==>-----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 172.7MiB
Copying blob 6e27b2228f9e done  
Copying blob 0b1b3a6418ad done  
Copying blob 2dc4374fcf9b done  
Copying blob 8041d704d7cd [===>----------------------------------] 12.0MiB / 121.9MiB
Copying blob 523e183c0552 [=======>------------------------------] 11.0MiB / 54.7MiB
Copying blob 6ccd2383e2e9 done  
```

will go on for many minutes before I control-c the command.  Those copies above were my attempt with

`podman push docker.io/gmontero/origin-docker-builder:latest` that I built on my system with `podman build..`

But I've tried it with others as well.

At the moment, here is the image listing on my fedora 32 laptop:

```
gmontero ~/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder  (fix-run-secrets-mount)$ podman images -a
REPOSITORY                                TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/gmontero/origin-docker-builder  latest  6063680b757c  7 minutes ago  366 MB
<none>                                    <none>  af8a83154185  7 minutes ago  366 MB
<none>                                    <none>  95343e29e8af  7 minutes ago  366 MB
<none>                                    <none>  4932f0c3e0ad  7 minutes ago  309 MB
<none>                                    <none>  8e240eb372c7  8 minutes ago  309 MB
<none>                                    <none>  e75ad42c1d80  8 minutes ago  309 MB
<none>                                    <none>  0fc759afd477  8 minutes ago  309 MB
<none>                                    <none>  0cf168058359  8 minutes ago  309 MB
<none>                                    <none>  7fb7f7ed72f5  9 minutes ago  181 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora         33      79fd58dc7611  2 months ago   181 MB
gmontero ~/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder  (fix-run-secrets-mount)$ 
```

I believe I'm at the latest podman based on `podman version` and checking the README for the latest version:

```
gmontero ~ $ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14.10
Built:        Tue Dec  8 09:37:43 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
gmontero ~ $
```

I've tried `podman system reset` as well as manual deletions like 

```
rm -rf ~/.config/containers ~/.local/share/containers
```




**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
gmontero ~ $ podman info --debug
host:

  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
gmontero ~ $ rpm -q podman
podman-2.2.1-1.fc32.x86_64
gmontero ~ $ 
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

running fedora 32 on my laptop ... so physical

There is no longer a ~/.config/containers dir nor ~/.local/containers on my system after the various `podman system reset` and the like.

If you all need detail from my `~/.local/share/containers/storage` that did not show up in `podman info --debug` please let me know

Also, when the `podman push` started failing, I started seeing this with my use of `df` with the complain of `df: /run/user/1000/doc: Operation not permitted` if I don't use `sudo`:

```
gmontero ~ $ df -m
df: /run/user/1000/doc: Operation not permitted
Filesystem              1M-blocks   Used Available Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs                    15720      0     15720   0% /dev
tmpfs                       15741    185     15557   2% /dev/shm
tmpfs                       15741      2     15739   1% /run
/dev/mapper/fedora-root     50141  15631     31934  33% /
tmpfs                       15741     25     15716   1% /tmp
/dev/nvme0n1p1                976    263       646  29% /boot
/dev/mapper/fedora-home    412695 148114    243549  38% /home
tmpfs                        3149      1      3149   1% /run/user/1000
gmontero ~ $ sudo df -m
Filesystem              1M-blocks   Used Available Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs                    15720      0     15720   0% /dev
tmpfs                       15741    186     15556   2% /dev/shm
tmpfs                       15741      2     15739   1% /run
/dev/mapper/fedora-root     50141  15631     31934  33% /
tmpfs                       15741     25     15716   1% /tmp
/dev/nvme0n1p1                976    263       646  29% /boot
/dev/mapper/fedora-home    412695 148114    243549  38% /home
tmpfs                        3149      1      3149   1% /run/user/1000
gmontero ~ $ 
```
`1000` is my uid

```
gmontero ~ $ id
uid=1000(gmontero) gid=1000(gmontero) groups=1000(gmontero),0(root),10(wheel),1001(docker) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023
gmontero ~ $ 
```

Some attempts with

```
gmontero ~ $ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
gmontero ~
```

had no benefit as well 

nor did reboots

do I need to uninstall / reininstall?

HELP

comments.body:
UPDATE:  push is working for me today.  No idea what change.  I was checking upload bandwidth yesterday via `speedtest-cli` and it was on par with what I have today.

I'm still curious about the `df: /run/user/1000/doc: Operation not permitted` error message when I run `df -m` as non-root.

Here is a recursive listing:

```
gmontero ~ $ ls -lR /run/user/1000/doc
/run/user/1000/doc:
total 0
dr-x------. 2 gmontero gmontero 0 Dec 31  1969 by-app

/run/user/1000/doc/by-app:
total 0
```

@vrothberg @mtrmac is that ^^ something you can speak to, or is it outside scope? fwiw @nalind and I talked about it a bit this morning and neither of us had an inkling

thanks
Looks like that's used by flatpak and applications that run under it, per https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/flatpak-command-reference.html#flatpak-document-export.

> Looks like that's used by flatpak and applications that run under it, per https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/flatpak-command-reference.html#flatpak-document-export.

Interesting ... a `ps -ef | grep flatpak` gets no hits, but something indirectly installed it for me:

```
gmontero ~ $ rpm -qa | grep flatpak
flatpak-selinux-1.8.5-1.fc32.noarch
flatpak-session-helper-1.8.5-1.fc32.x86_64
flatpak-1.8.5-1.fc32.x86_64
flatpak-libs-1.8.5-1.fc32.x86_64
gmontero ~ $ 
```
`flatpak list --app` will tell you which applications you have installed as flatpaks, but I think it's safe to say the directory and its contents aren't related to podman.

> `flatpak list --app` will tell you which applications you have installed as flatpaks, but I think it's safe to say the directory and its contents aren't related to podman.

thanks @nalind 

turns out I get 

```
gmontero ~ $ flatpak list --app
gmontero ~ $ 
```

that said, yes on the non-podman characterization ... will deal with it separately / with impunity given it is not podman related :-)

I'll close this out, at least for now, and track how my pushes do (both from emacs and non-emacs windows, though it is working in both)

thanks for the help

Hum, as of 2.2.0 the code that manages retries was changed supposedly make the decision to retry visible in Podman output, and to include the error that caused the previous attempt to fail.

But, more importantly, AFAICS the code currently _never_ retries on pushes. So I’m almost tempted to say that this is something outside Podman, not that I have any idea how that could happen (and in that case I would also expect _some_ failure report).
